page: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, utilizing the AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz. The CPU includes 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 20 MB L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core.

For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 316 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 3 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the high-frequency AMD processor delivers top-tier performance in single-core workloads, including stress-ng single-core CPU tests, Geekbench single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, because the instance is configured with only 4 vCPUs, multi-core benchmarks—such as stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression—fall into the average tier.

The high memory density of 32.0 GB per vCPU makes this instance highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute scaling. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both small and large blocks perform in the average tier, as do database operations and Redis SET benchmarks. For machine learning, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds with small 135M parameter LLM models, but struggles with larger models, as evidenced by poor prompt processing speeds on 70B parameter models. This hardware profile is best suited for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, caching layers, and single-threaded data processing tasks requiring fast local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, providing a high ratio of 32.0 GB per core, and includes a 316 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and text processing, while multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks remain in the average tier. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications that do not require high parallel compute scaling. It is well-suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 316 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 316 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive tasks.
tagline: High-frequency single-core processing paired with extreme memory density and fast local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 vCPUs operating at 5.0 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Bundled 316 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-bound processing
